title: dianthus repens subsp. repens
intro: Dianthus repens subsp. repens, also known as Creeping Pink or Pink Flowered Pink, is a low-growing, spreading perennial plant that belongs to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which make it a valuable remedy for various health issues. The plant's medicinal properties are said to promote wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive problems, while its health benefits include reducing stress, improving sleep, and boosting the immune system. Dianthus repens subsp. repens can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves, making it a versatile and accessible natural remedy.
